En: Lots of people out there miss the good old days.
Zh: “很多人都怀念过去的美好时光。”

En: You think it's gonna be much longer?
Zh: “你觉得还会不会很久？”

En: People are starting to think, waking up.
Zh: “大家都开始有想法了，快醒醒。”

En: Don't you ever wish Hjelmdall was a real place?
Zh: “你有没有希望过赫姆达尔是个真实的地方？”

En: So many things happening *behind* the scenes.
Zh: “*幕后*发生了好多事情。”

En: People are just scared of the truth.
Zh: “大家只是害怕真相而已。”

En: I'm just saying, as a *Revacholian*, I am slightly worried.
Zh: “我只是想说，作为一个*瑞瓦科人*，我稍微有些担心。”

En: What about Revacholian traditions and customs?
Zh: “瑞瓦科人的传统和风俗呢？”

En: Gotta be careful what you say these days.
Zh: “现在说话都要小心咯。”

En: Quite colourful in here... I mean the lights!
Zh: “这里颜色挺丰富的……我是说灯光！”

En: I don't mean to sound paranoid or anything, *but*...
Zh: “我不想让自己听起来像是个偏执狂或者什么的，*但是*……”

En: It's not like I go to meetings or anything.
Zh: “我也不是去参加集会啊或者什么的。”

En: Mass-radio can't be trusted. They're all just mouth-pieces.
Zh: “主流媒体根本不可信。他们都是传话机。”

En: If I come to *your* home I respect your way of life, right?
Zh: “如果我去*你*家，就该尊重你的生活方式，是吧？”

En: Looks like an abandoned building...
Zh: “看起来像是一栋废弃的建筑……”

En: Good lord...
It's a bit crowded in here.
Zh: “这里有点拥挤。”

En: Are you enjoying yourself, darling?
Zh: “你玩的开心吗，亲爱的？”

En: There was a confirmed sighting in Mirova in December.
Zh: “已经确认12月在米诺瓦出现过。”

En: The evidence is right there -- we can't just ignore it.
Zh: “证据就摆在这儿——不能就这么无视它吧。”

En: An *unconfirmed* sighting is just a story.
Zh: “没有*真正*目击到的话，那就只是个故事了。”

En: C'mon, Gary, it's never that black and white.
Zh: “拜托，盖理，从来都不是黑白分明这么简单的。”

En: God, I hope the traps will hold...
Zh: “老天，真希望那个陷阱能坚持住……”

En: Hate to break it to you, Gary, but rhinos don't just combust in flames.
Zh: “盖理，我很不想打断你，但是犀牛是不会在火焰中燃烧的。”

En: You could ask the Miss what she thinks. Later, if you have the time -- though you doubt she'll tell you much at this point.
Zh: 你应该问问那位女士的看法。之后，如果你有时间的话——虽然你怀疑她对此没什么能说的。

En: Damn, I felt *really* strange just now.
Zh: “该死，我刚才*真的*有种奇怪的感觉。”

En: Mhm. No further response. All his concentration is on the contraption.
Zh: “嗯。”没有更多的回应。他的全部精力都集中在那个奇妙的装置上。

En: The water this side of the peninsula is cleaner, actually. Smells a little salty.
Zh: 其实，半岛这一侧的水要清澈很多。闻起来有点咸味。

En: A metal-and-plastic contraption bobs up and down amidst the trembling reeds. At first it just looks like trash, but if you look closer...
Zh: 一个金属塑料制成的奇妙装置在摇摆的芦苇丛中若隐若现。一开始看起来像是垃圾，但是当你靠近一看……

En: Let the empty buoy be. [Leave.]
Zh: 不管空浮标了。[离开。]

En: Let the buoy be. [Leave.]
Zh: 别管这个浮标了。[离开。]

En: That over there, he points to the strange-looking object, "must be the buoy Klaasje told us about -- the one she hid her passport in. We should take a look."
Zh: “看那边，”他指着一个奇怪的东西，“肯定是卡拉洁告诉我们的浮标——就是她藏护照的地方。我们应该去看看。”

En: Oooh, hidden things -- secrets, lies!
Zh: 噢噢噢噢，藏起来的东西——秘密，谎言！

En: Cold. Air. Tendons. Snapped the cords keeping the buoy in place. A little longer and it would be floating somewhere out in the open sea...
Zh: 寒冷。空气。肌腱。割裂了绑住浮标的绳索。如果再长一点，它就会飘到远海的某个地方去了……

En: Yes, spindly extremities have been here, snapped the cords keeping the buoy in place. A little longer -- and it will be floating somewhere out in the open sea...
Zh: 是的，纤弱的四肢曾经来过这里，割裂了绑住浮标的绳索。如果再长一点——它就会飘到远海上的某个地方去了……

En: Pick up the buoy.
Zh: 捡起浮标。

En: Open the buoy. (Move on.)
Zh: 打开浮标。（继续。）

En: This must be the buoy Klaasje mentioned.
Zh: 这一定就是卡拉洁提到的浮标。

En: We're lucky it's still here. A little longer and it would have floated away...
Zh: “它还在这里，算我们走运。要是再久一点，说不定就飘走了……”

En: No documents. Who do you think took them?
Zh: “没有文件。你觉得是谁拿走的？”

En: Stare into the empty buoy.
Zh: 盯着空浮标。

En: The buoy continues to be mostly empty.
Zh: 浮标里还是空空如也。

En: I have no idea. The lieutenant taps his foot, frowning.
Zh: “我不知道。”警督皱着眉头，跺了跺脚。

En: Maybe Klaasje took them herself...
Zh: “也许是卡拉洁自己拿走的……”

En: I think there's a cosmic conspiracy -- identities being lost and stolen left and right...
Zh: “我觉得里面有个大阴谋——身份证明丢失，到处偷来摸去……”

En: Could this be related to the cryptozoologists and their little expedition?
Zh: “这会不会跟神秘动物学家和他们的小探险有关？”

En: This is a minor quirk. We know what was in the buoy anyway.
Zh: “这只是个很不上道的怪癖。反正我们知道浮标里藏了什么。”

En: He purses his lips. "Or *think* we do... This is a small loose end, either way. Not important I hope."
Zh: 他咬紧双唇。“或者*以为*我们如此……不管怎么说，这里留了个小悬念。希望不会太关键。”

En: This wasn't a *real* identity, at least not as far as we know. He sighs. "We should go."
Zh: “这不是*真正*的身份证明，至少据我们所知不是。”他叹了口气。“我们该走了。”

En: That may very well be the case. We should keep an eye on her. He sighs. "Nothing more for us to do here. Let's go."
Zh: “情况很可能就是这样。我们应该盯着她点。”他叹了口气。“我们没什么能做的了，走吧。”

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Enough. Shake it off.
Zh: 够了。别想了。

En: Moves across the water, leaving no traces. You're cold again, as always...
Zh: 漂洋过海，不留痕迹。像往常一样，你又感觉到寒冷了……

En: It's just rapid movement across the water. It leaves no trace. A whisper...
Zh: 它只是在水面上快速的移动而已，没有留下痕迹。一声耳语……

En: Yes, but who or what is this?
Zh: 是的，但是到底是谁，或者是什么？

En: Splendour.
Zh: 辉煌壮丽。

En: Indeed. The lieutenant nods thoughtfully. "Let's see what's inside."
Zh: “的确。”警督若有所思的点点头。“我们看看里面有什么。”

En: The lieutenant flashes a mirthless half-smile. "We still got here too late. There's nothing of use here anymore."
Zh: 警督脸上闪过一丝苦笑。“我们来的太晚了。这里已经没什么有用的东西了。”

En: Examine the plastic ball.
Zh: 检查塑料球。

En: Shake the buoy.
Zh: 摇一下浮标。

En: Sniff the buoy.
Zh: 闻闻浮标。

En: The buoy is not tied to anything -- the cords dangling from the bottom really have been torn or cut. You lift it out of the water easily, noticing how light it is.
Zh: 浮标没有系在任何东西上面——底部悬挂的绳索像是被扯开或者割断了。你很容易就把它从水里提了起来，感觉轻飘飘的。

En: You lift the buoy out of the water without much effort. It's not tied to anything -- the cords dangling from the bottom appear to have been cut.
Zh: 你没费太大力气就把浮标提出了水面。它没有系在什么东西上面——底部悬挂的绳索像是被割断了。

En: The number '11' has been written on the yellow plastic. It hasn't been in the water for very long, but it's already discoloured and slimy with silt. A latch holds it closed, but only just barely -- the brittle metal of the latch has cracked.
Zh: 黄色塑料上写着数字“11”。它在水里泡了不是很久，但却已经有些褪色，上面附着的淤泥黏糊糊的。浮标是关上的，不过很勉强——闩锁的脆弱金属裂开了。

En: There's something in there... splashing around.
Zh: 里面有什么东西……溅得到处都是。

En: It smells like you would expect it to smell: a concentrated version of the coast. Salt, industrial slop and decay.
Zh: 它的味道跟你期待的没什么不同：海岸味道的高度浓缩版。咸味，工业废水味，还有腐烂的味道。

En: A shot-glass's worth of seawater pours out -- some algae. And nothing else.
Zh: 一玻璃杯量的海水倾泄而出——有些海藻。除此之外别无其他。

En: Simple construction, very unsafe.
Zh: “结构很简单，非常不安全。”

En: Primitive construction, but it does the job.
Zh: 很原始的装置，不过也算派上用场了。

En: ...and just a dash of hard liquor. Yummm.
Zh: ……而且还有一丁点烈酒的味道。美味。

En: Well, damn.
Zh: “该死。”

En: Another identity misplaced... There must be some cosmic conspiracy afoot!
Zh: 又是身份错位……一定在进行什么大阴谋！

En: It must have been Klaasje's enemies -- which means she's in danger!
Zh: “肯定是卡拉洁的敌人——也就是说，她现在很危险！”

En: Could be. We should keep an eye out. He sighs. "Nothing more for us to do here. Let's go."
Zh: “也许。我们应该留心一点。”他叹了口气。“我们没什么能做的了，走吧。”

En: No answers appear to you in the dirty sea water at the bottom of the buoy. Just the heavy smell of brine and silt and disappearance.
Zh: 在浮标底部肮脏的海水里，你找不到任何答案。只不过海水和淤泥浓厚的味道消失了。

En: This is a disaster. Everything is ruined.
Zh: “这是个灾难。一切都毁了。”

En: I wouldn't go so far as to say that. This is only a small loose end. But I do wish we knew who took the documents.
Zh: “我倒不会这么说。这不过是个小悬念。不过我确实想知道是谁拿走了文件。”

En: I've no idea. Do you have any theories, lieutenant?
Zh: “我不知道。你有什么看法吗，警督。”

En: Might have been Klaasje. Might have been the people after her. Might have been a local drunk. He shrugs. "We should go."
Zh: “也许是卡拉洁。也许是追查她的人。又或许是本地的一个醉汉。”他耸耸肩。“我们该走了。”

En: Yes, the cryptid has always wanted to be an Annouk Meijer-Smit. He sighs. "We should go."
Zh: “是的，神秘动物一直想变成安诺・梅耶尔-斯密特。”他叹了口气。“我们该走了。”

En: In my experience, cryptids are generally uninterested in travel documentation. He sighs. "We should go."
Zh: “以我的经验来看，神秘动物一般对旅行证明文件没什么兴趣。”他叹了口气。“我们该走了。”

En: There's a cosmic conspiracy -- identities being lost and stolen left and right...
Zh: 这里肯定有大阴谋——身份证明丢失，到处偷来摸去……

En: I think... it may have been the Insulindian phasmid.
Zh: 我觉得……可能是东南亚竹节虫。

En: It must have been Klaasje's enemies -- which means she's in danger!
Zh: 肯定是卡拉洁的敌人——也就是说，她现在很危险！

En: Maybe Klaasje took them herself...
Zh: 也许是卡拉洁自己拿走的……

En: Don't try to guess who took the documents. You don't have enough information.
Zh: 别再猜到底是谁拿走文件的了。现在手上掌握的信息还不够。

En: Coward. You should *always* try to guess.
Zh: 胆小鬼。你就应该*一直*猜下去。

En: The dome-shaped plastic buoy bobs up and down in the water amidst the trembling reeds. The ajar latch still exhibits the emptiness within.
Zh: 圆顶型的塑料浮标在水里浮浮沉沉，四周环绕着摇摆的芦苇。半开的闩锁仍然展示着内在的空虚。

En: The dome-shaped plastic buoy still bobs up and down in the water amidst the trembling reeds.
Zh: 圆顶型的塑料浮标在水面上浮浮沉沉，四周环绕着摇摆的芦苇。

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: The male is... condishoned reflexes... inc...apable of mentally free response...
Zh: “男性……从条件反射上说……不能……做出精神上的自由反应……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: The male tries to affirm his *Manhood* by being like... all other men...
Zh: “男性试着确认自己的*男子气概*，通过表现地……像其他男人一样……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: The male is psy... psysh... psychically passive...
Zh: “男性的精……精神上是被动的……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: The male is... interch... changeable with every other male...
Zh: “男性……可以……与其他任何男性互换……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: A *male artist* is a contradiction in terms...
Zh: “*男艺术家*这个词明显是自相矛盾的……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: I am a turd, a lowly abject turd!
Zh: “我是个混蛋，卑微可怜的混蛋！”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: Dropping out is... not the answer... fucking-up is...
Zh: “退出……不是答案……愚蠢才是……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: A degenerate... can only produce degenerate *art*...
Zh: “一个堕落者……只能创作出堕落的*艺术品*……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: The male is an... incomplete female...
Zh: “男性是……不完整的女性……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: The male... has no deep-seated individuality...
Zh: “男性……根本没有深层的个性……”

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: *hiccup*
Zh: *打嗝*

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: *hurk*
Zh: *打嗝*

ConvTitle: COAST / WOMENS RIGHTS second stage barks
En: *snort*
Zh: *哼哼*
